steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content: For $5x^2+10x+16=0$, the discriminant is $$b^2-4ac=10^2-4(5)(16)=100-320=-220$$ A negative discriminant means the parabola never crosses the $x$-axis, so the equation has no real solutions.

steps.title: Confirm with a graph
steps.content: Graph $y=5x^2+10x+16$ in Desmos. Its minimum is above the $x$-axis, so there are no $x$-intercepts. Therefore the equation has $0$ distinct real solutions, which matches choice D.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choices A, B, and C all claim at least one real solution, but the discriminant is negative. Choice D is the only answer that gives $0$ real solutions, so D is correct.
